sourceTitle: Preserve perfect meeting memory with AI Meeting Notes

questions.question: How do you initially add an AI Meeting Notes block to a Notion page?

options.text: Type "/meet" or click the “Meet” pill at the bottom of a new page
options.explanation: Both typing “/meet” and clicking the “Meet” pill insert the AI Meeting Notes block.

options.text: Press Ctrl+M on any page
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for inserting AI Meeting Notes.

options.text: Upload a .txt file containing the notes
options.explanation: AI Meeting Notes is a block, not a file upload feature.

options.text: Enable it in the workspace settings under "Integrations"
options.explanation: The block is added directly on a page, not via a global integration toggle.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of attaching AI Meeting Notes to a Notion calendar event?

options.text: It automatically records the video of the meeting
options.explanation: AI Meeting Notes does not record video; it captures transcription and notes.

options.text: The notes block appears automatically each time you create a new page for that event
options.explanation: When enabled, AI Meeting Notes auto‑adds a notes block to new pages linked to calendar events.

options.text: It syncs the meeting to Google Calendar
options.explanation: Syncing to Google Calendar is not part of the described feature.

options.text: It sends a reminder email to all participants before the meeting starts
options.explanation: Reminder emails are not mentioned as a function of AI Meeting Notes.

questions.question: Which meeting format option would you select for a quick daily stand‑up?

options.text: Sales
options.explanation: The Sales format is tailored for client‑facing conversations, not stand‑ups.

options.text: Team Meeting
options.explanation: Team Meeting is more generic; there is a dedicated Stand‑up format.

options.text: Stand‑up
options.explanation: The Stand‑up format is designed for short, recurring check‑ins.

options.text: Auto
options.explanation: Auto lets AI decide, but the explicit Stand‑up option is best for daily stand‑ups.

questions.question: Why is it recommended to add AI Meeting Notes to your database templates?

options.text: So the AI can automatically delete old notes after 30 days
options.explanation: Automatic deletion isn’t mentioned; the benefit is about consistency.

options.text: It ensures recurring meetings like 1:1s always have a ready‑to‑use notes page
options.explanation: Embedding the block in templates means every recurring event starts with a notes page.

options.text: It disables the transcription feature for privacy reasons
options.explanation: Adding to templates does not affect transcription settings.

options.text: It forces all participants to use the same font style in their notes
options.explanation: Font styling isn’t a function of the AI Meeting Notes block.

questions.question: What happens when you @‑mention a teammate in the Notes tab during a meeting?

options.text: Notion AI highlights the mention but does not include it in the summary
options.explanation: The AI actually uses the mention to create clear next‑step items in the summary.

options.text: The mention is ignored unless the person also speaks during the meeting
options.explanation: Mentions are processed regardless of whether the person speaks.

options.text: Notion AI automatically tags that person in the generated summary as an owner of the action item
options.explanation: Tagging in notes lets the AI turn the mention into an assigned action in the summary.

options.text: It creates a separate task in the user’s personal task list
options.explanation: The AI only tags in the summary; it does not auto‑create a task unless you set up further automation.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the “auto‑share” feature mentioned in the guide?

options.text: It shares the meeting notes with everyone in the workspace immediately after the meeting ends
options.explanation: Auto‑share refers to automatically adding the notes block to new pages, not instant sharing with the whole workspace.

options.text: It automatically adds the AI Meeting Notes block to each new meeting page based on your calendar settings
options.explanation: Auto‑share (and auto‑add) means the block appears automatically in new meeting pages.

options.text: It posts a summary of the meeting to a Slack channel without user input
options.explanation: Posting to Slack requires a separate automation; auto‑share is about block insertion.

options.text: It syncs the meeting transcript to Google Docs for offline editing
options.explanation: Syncing to Google Docs is not part of the described functionality.

questions.question: How can you use Notion AI after a meeting ends to streamline follow‑up communication?

options.text: Ask the AI to draft follow‑up emails or status updates based on the meeting notes
options.explanation: The guide states AI can draft follow‑up emails, project updates, etc., using the captured notes.

options.text: Enable the AI to automatically send calendar invites for the next meeting
options.explanation: Sending calendar invites is not a post‑meeting AI capability described.

options.text: Use AI to delete the meeting transcript to save storage space
options.explanation: Deletion isn’t mentioned as an AI function after the meeting.

options.text: Have the AI export the transcript to a PDF and email it to all participants
options.explanation: Exporting to PDF isn’t listed; the focus is on drafting textual follow‑ups.

questions.question: What is a key benefit of linking meeting notes to a projects database via Relation and Rollup properties?

options.text: It automatically updates project timelines based on meeting discussions
options.explanation: Relation and Rollup surface linked data; they don’t auto‑adjust timelines.

options.text: It allows you to view meeting decisions directly within related project pages, keeping context in one place
options.explanation: Relations let you connect notes to projects, and Rollups surface relevant details, preserving context.

options.text: It creates a backup of the meeting audio in the projects database
options.explanation: Audio backup isn’t part of the Relation/Rollup functionality.

options.text: It disables the AI’s ability to generate summaries for those meetings
options.explanation: Linking does not affect AI summarization; it enhances accessibility of the summary.

questions.question: If you forget to attach AI Meeting Notes to a calendar event, how can you still launch the notes during a live Zoom call?

options.text: Open the Notion desktop app and manually type "/meet" in a blank page
options.explanation: While you could create a new page, the guide mentions a quicker method via the menu bar.

options.text: Use the menu bar at the top of your screen to launch AI Meeting Notes directly
options.explanation: The guide states you can launch the notes from the top‑screen menu bar even if not attached to the event.

options.text: Send a DM to a teammate asking them to share the notes link
options.explanation: This circumvents the built‑in shortcut described.

options.text: Click the “Record” button in Zoom to start transcription, then import it into Notion later
options.explanation: Zoom recording isn’t part of the AI Meeting Notes workflow.

questions.question: Which statement best captures the overall purpose of Notion’s AI Meeting Notes as described in the guide?

options.text: To replace human note‑takers entirely and eliminate the need for any meeting preparation
options.explanation: The tool assists, but preparation like agenda setting is still recommended.

options.text: To streamline capture, summarization, and post‑meeting actions so participants can stay present and act on decisions efficiently
options.explanation: This aligns with the guide’s emphasis on presence, clear summaries, and actionable follow‑ups.

options.text: To provide a video recording of every meeting for later review
options.explanation: Video recording is not a feature of AI Meeting Notes.

options.text: To automatically assign tasks to all meeting attendees without user input
options.explanation: Tasks are suggested via tagging; automatic assignment requires user action.
